author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter III. 6–21.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We observe, beloved, that the intimation with which we yesterday excited your attention has brought you together with more alacrity, and in greater number than usual; but meanwhile let us, if you please, pay our debt of a discourse on the Gospel Lesson, which comes in due course.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You shall then hear, beloved, as well what we have already effected concerning the peace of the Church, and what we hope yet further to accomplish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the present, then, let the whole attention of your hearts be given to the gospel; let none be thinking of anything else.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he who attends to it wholly apprehends with difficulty, must not he who divides himself by diverse thoughts let go what he has received?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, you remember, beloved, that on the last Lord’s day, as the Lord deigned to help us, we discoursed of spiritual regeneration.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That lesson we have caused to be read to you again, so that what was then left unspoken, we may now, by the aid of your prayers in the name of Christ, fulfill.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Spiritual regeneration is one, just as the generation of the flesh is one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Nicodemus said the truth when he said to the Lord that a man cannot, when he is old, return again into his mother’s womb and be born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He indeed said that a man cannot do this when he is old, as if he could do it even were he an infant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But be he fresh from the womb, or now in years, he cannot possibly return again into the mother’s bowels and be born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But just as for the birth of the flesh, the bowels of woman avail to bring forth the child only once, so for the spiritual birth the bowels of the Church avail that a man be baptized only once.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, in case one should say, “Well, but this man was born in heresy, and this in schism:” all that was cut away, if you remember what was debated to you about our three fathers, of whom God willed to be called the God, not that they were thus alone but because in them alone the figure of the future people was made up in its completeness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we find one born of a bond woman disinherited, one born of a free woman made heir: again, we find one born of a free woman disinherited, one born of a bond woman made heir.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ishmael, born of a bond woman, disinherited; Isaac, born of a free woman, made heir: Esau, born of a free woman, disinherited; the sons of Jacob, born of bond women, made heirs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, in these three fathers the figure of the whole future people is seen: and not without reason God saith, “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob: this,” saith He, “is my name for ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rather let us remember what was promised to Abraham himself: for this was promised to Isaac, and also to Jacob.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do we find?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In thy seed shall all nations be blessed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At that time the one man believed what as yet he saw not: men now see, and are blinded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was promised to the one man is fulfilled in the nations; and they who will not see what is already fulfilled, are separating themselves from the communion of the nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what avails it them that they will not see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See they do, whether they will or no; the open truth strikes against their closed eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was in answer to Nicodemus, who was of them that had believed on Jesus, that it was said, And Jesus did not trust Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To certain men, indeed, He did not trust Himself, though they had already believed on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus it is written, “Many believed in His name, seeing the signs which He did.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Jesus did not trust Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He needed not that any should testify of man; for Himself knew what was in man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, they already believed on Jesus, and yet Jesus did not trust Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why? because they were not yet born again of water and of the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this have we ex horted and do exhort our brethren the catechumens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if you ask them, they have already believed in Jesus; but because they have not yet received His flesh and blood, Jesus has not yet trusted Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What must they do that Jesus may trust Himself to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They must be born again of water and of the Spirit; the Church that is in travail with them must bring them forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They have been conceived; they must be brought forth to the light: they have breasts to be nourished at; let them not fear lest, being born, they may be smothered; let them not depart from the mother’s breasts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No man can return into his mother’s bowels and be born again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But some one is born of a bond woman?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, did they who were born of bond women at the former time, return into the wombs of the free to be born anew?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The seed of Abraham was in Ishmael also; but that Abraham might have a son of the bond maid, it was at the advice of his wife.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The child was of the husband’s seed, not of the womb, but at the sole pleasure of the wife.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was his birth of a bond woman the reason why he was disinherited?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, if he was disinherited because he was the son of a bond woman, no sons of bond women would be admitted to the inheritance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The sons of Jacob were admitted to the inheritance; but Ishmael was put out of it, not because born of a bond woman, but because he was proud to his mother, proud to his mother’s son; for his mother was Sarah rather than Hagar.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one gave her womb, the other’s will was added: Abraham would not have done what Sarah willed not: therefore was he Sarah’s son rather.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because he was proud to his brother, proud in playing, that is, in mocking him; what said Sarah?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Cast out the bond woman and her son; for the son of the bond woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not, therefore, the bowels of the bond woman that caused his rejection, but the slave’s neck.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the free-born is a slave if he is proud, and, what is worse, the slave of a bad mistress, of pride itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, my brethren, answer the man, that a man cannot be born a second time; answer fearlessly, that a man cannot be born a second time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever is done a second time is mockery, whatever is done a second time is play.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is Ishmael playing, let him be cast out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Sarah observed them playing, saith the Scripture, and said to Abraham, “Cast out the bond woman and her son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The playing of the boys displeased Sarah.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She saw something strange in their play.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not they who have sons like to see them playing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She saw and disapproved it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Something or other she saw in their play; she saw mockery in it, observed the pride of the slave; she was displeased with it, and she cast him out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The children of bond women, when wicked, are cast out; and the child of the free woman, when an Esau, is cast out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let none, therefore, presume on his birth of good parents; let none presume on his being baptized by holy men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him that is baptized by holy men still beware lest he be not a Jacob, but an Esau.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This would I say then, brethren, it is better to be baptized by men that seek their own and love the world, which is what the name of bond woman imports, and to be spiritually seeking the inheritance of Christ, so as to be as it were a son of Jacob by a bond woman, than to be baptized by holy men and to become proud, so as to be an Esau to be cast out, though born of a free woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hold ye this fast, brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are not coaxing you, let none of your hope be in us; we flatter neither ourselves nor you; every man bears his own burden.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is our duty to speak, that we be not judged unhappily: yours to hear, and that with the heart, lest what we give be required of you; nay, that when it is required, it may be found a gain, not a loss.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord says to Nicodemus, and explains to him: “Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou, says He, understandest a carnal generation, when thou sayest, Can a man return into his mother’s bowels?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The birth for the kingdom of God must be of water and of the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If one is born to the temporal inheritance of a human father, be he born of the bowels of a carnal mother; if one is born to the everlasting inheritance of God as his Father, be he born of the bowels of the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A father, as one that will die, begets a son by his wife to succeed him; but God begets of the Church sons, not to succeed Him, but to abide with Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He goes on: “That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are born spiritually then, and spirit we are born by the word and sacrament.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Spirit is present that we may be born; the Spirit is invisibly present whereof thou art born, for thou too must be invisibly born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He goes on to say: “Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Spirit bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest its voice, but knowest not whence it cometh, or whither it goeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: None sees the Spirit; and how do we hear the Spirit’s voice?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There sounds a psalm, it is the Spirit’s voice; the gospel sounds, it is the Spirit’s voice; the divine word sounds, it is the Spirit’s voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thou hearest its voice, and knowest not whence it cometh, and whither it goeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou art born of the Spirit, thou too shall be so, that one who is not born of the Spirit knows not, as for thee, whence thou comest, or whither thou goest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He said, as He went on, “So is also every one that is born of the Spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Nicodemus answered and said unto Him, How can these things be?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, in fact, in the carnal sense, he knew not how.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In him occurred what the Lord had said; the Spirit’s voice he heard, but knew not whence it came, and whither it was going.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered and said unto him, Art thou a master in Israel, and knowest not these things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh, brethren! what? do we think that the Lord meant to taunt scornfully this master of the Jews?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord knew what He was doing; He wished the man to be born of the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No man is born of the Spirit if he be not humble, for humility itself makes us to be born of the Spirit; “for the Lord is nigh to them that are of broken heart.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man was puffed up with his mastership, and it appeared of some importance to himself that he was a teacher of the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus pulled down his pride, that he might be born of the Spirit: He taunted him as an unlearned man; not that the Lord wished to appear his superior.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What comparison can there be, God compared to man, truth to falsehood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ greater than Nicodemus!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ought this to be said, can it be said, is it to be thought?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it were said, “Christ is greater than angels,” it were ridiculous: for incomparably greater than every creature is He by whom every creature was made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet He rallies the man on his pride: “Art thou a master in Israel, and knowest not these things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if He said, Behold, thou knowest nothing, thou art a proud chief; be thou born of the Spirit: for if thou be born of the Spirit, thou wilt keep the ways of God, so as to follow Christ’s humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, indeed, is He high above all angels, that, “being in the form of God, He thought it not robbery to be equal with God, but emptied Himself, taking upon Him the form of a servant, being made into the likeness of men, and found in fashion as a man: He humbled Himself, being made obedient unto death” (and lest any kind of death should please thee), “even the death of the cross.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He hung on the cross, and they scoffed at Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He could have come down from the cross; but He deferred, that He might rise again from the tomb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, the Lord, bore with proud slaves; the physician with the sick.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He did this, how ought they to act whom it behoves to be born of the Spirit!—if He did this, He who is the true Master in heaven, not of men only, but also of angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the angels are learned, they are so by the Word of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they are learned by the Word of God, ask of what they are learned; and you shall find, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The neck of man is done away with, only the hard and stiff neck, that it may be gentle to bear the yoke of Christ, of which it is said, “My yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He goes on, “If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not; how shall ye believe, if I tell you heavenly things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What earthly things did He tell, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Except a man be born again;” is that an earthly thing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Spirit bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest its voice, and knowest not whence it cometh, or whither it goeth;” is that earthly?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He spoke it of the wind, as some have understood it, when they were asked what earthly thing the Lord meant, when He said, “If I told you earthly things, and ye believe not; how shall ye believe, if I tell you heavenly things?”—when, I say, it was asked of certain men what “earthly thing” the Lord meant, being in difficulty, they said, What He said, “The Spirit bloweth where it listeth,” and “its voice thou hearest, and knowest not whence it cometh, or whither it goeth,” He said concerning the wind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now what did He name earthly?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was speaking of the spiritual birth; and going on, saith, “So is every one that is born of the Spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, brethren, which of us does not see, for example, the south wind going from south to north, or another wind coming from east to west?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, know we not whence it cometh and whither it goeth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What earthly thing, then, did He tell, which men did not believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it that which He had said about raising the temple again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely, for He had received His body of the earth, and that earth taken of the earthly body He was preparing to raise up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They did not believe Him as about to raise up earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If I told you earthly things,” saith He, “and ye believe not; how shall ye believe if I tell you heavenly things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is, if ye believe not that I can raise up the temple cast down by you, how shall ye believe that men can be regenerated by the Spirit?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He goes on: “And no man hath ascended into heaven, but He that came down from heaven, the Son of man who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, He was here, and was also in heaven; was here in His flesh, in heaven by His divinity; yea, everywhere by His divinity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Born of a mother, not quitting the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Two nativities of Christ are understood: one divine, the other human: one, that by which we were to be made; the other, that by which we were to be made anew: both marvellous; that without mother, this without father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because He had taken a body of Adam,—for Mary was of Adam,—and was about to raise that same body again, it was an earthly thing He had said in saying, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this was a heavenly thing, when He said, “Except a man be born again of water and of the Spirit, he shall not see the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come then, brethren!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God has willed to be the Son of man; and willed men to be sons of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came down for our sakes; let us ascend for His sake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He alone descended and ascended, He who saith, “No man hath ascended into heaven, but He who came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are they not therefore to ascend into heaven whom He makes sons of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly they are: this is the promise to us, “They shall be equal to the angels of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then how is it that no man ascends, but He that descended?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because one only descended, only one ascends.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What of the rest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are we to understand, but that they shall be His members, that one may ascend?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore it follows that “no man hath ascended into heaven, but He who came down from heaven, the Son of man who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou marvel that He was both here and in heaven?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such He made His disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle Paul saying, “But our conversation is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Apostle Paul, a man, walked in the flesh on earth, and yet had his conversation in heaven, was the God of heaven and earth not able to be both in heaven and on earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, if none but He descended and ascended, what hope is there for the rest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The hope for the rest is this, that He came down in order that in Him and with Him they might be one, who should ascend through Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He saith not, And to seeds,” saith the apostle, “as in many; but as in one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to believers he saith, “And ye are Christ’s; and if Christ’s, then are Abraham’s seed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What he said to be one, that he said that we all are.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, in the Psalms, many sometimes sing, to show that one is made of many; sometimes one sings, to show what is made of many.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore was it only one that was healed in the pool; and whoever else went down into it was not healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now this one shows forth the oneness of the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Woe to them who hate unity, and make to themselves parties among men!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them hear him who wished to make them one, in one, for one: let them hear him who says, Be not ye making many: “I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But neither he that planteth is anything, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were saying, “I am of Paul, I of Apollos, I of Cephas.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he says, “Is Christ divided?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be ye in one, be one thing, be one person: “No man hath ascended into heaven, but He who came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo! we wish to be thine, they said to Paul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he said to them, I will not that ye be Paul’s, but be ye His whose is Paul together with you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He came down and died, and by that death delivered us from death: being slain by death, He slew death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And you know, brethren, that this death entered into the world through the devil’s envy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “God made not death,” saith the Scripture, “nor delights He in the destruction of the living; but He created all things to be.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what saith it here?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But by the devil’s envy, death entered into the whole world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the death offered for our entertainment by the devil, man would not come by constraint; for the devil had not the power of forcing, but only cunning to persuade.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hadst thou not consented, the devil had brought in nothing: thy own consenting, O man, led thee to death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the mortal are mortals born; from immortals we are become mortals.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From Adam all men are mortal; but Jesus the Son of God, the Word of God, by which all things were made, the only Son equal with the Father, was made mortal: “for the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He endured death, then; but death He hanged on the cross, and mortal men are delivered from death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord calls to mind a great matter, which was done in a figure with them of old: “And as Moses,” saith He, “lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of man be lifted up; that every one who believeth on Him may not perish, but have everlasting life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great mystery is here, as they who read know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, let them hear, as well they who have not read as they who have forgotten what perhaps they had heard or read.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The people Israel were fallen helplessly in the wilderness by the bite of serpents; they suffered a great calamity by many deaths: for it was the stroke of God correcting and scourging them that He might instruct them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this was shown a great mystery, the figure of a thing to come: the Lord Himself testifies in this passage, so that no man can give another interpretation than that which the truth indicates concerning itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Moses was ordered by the Lord to make a brazen serpent, and to raise it on a pole in the wilderness, and to admonish the people Israel, that, when any had been bitten by a serpent, he should look to that serpent raised up on the pole.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was done: men were bitten; they looked and were healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are the biting serpents?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sins, from the mortality of the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the serpent lifted up?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord’s death on the cross.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as death came by the serpent, it was figured by the image of a serpent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The serpent’s bite was deadly, the Lord’s death is life-giving.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A serpent is gazed on that the serpent may have no power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A death is gazed on, that death may have no power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But whose death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The death of life: if it may be said, the death of life; ay, for it may be said, but said wonderfully.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But should it not be spoken, seeing it was a thing to be done?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall I hesitate to utter that which the Lord has deigned to do for me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is not Christ the life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet Christ hung on the cross.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is not Christ life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet Christ was dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in Christ’s death, death died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Life dead slew death; the fullness of life swallowed up death; death was absorbed in the body of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also shall we say in the resurrection, when now triumphant we shall sing, “Where, O death, is thy contest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where, O death, is thy sting?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Meanwhile brethren, that we may be healed from sin, let us now gaze on Christ crucified; for “as Moses,” saith He, “lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of man be lifted up; that whosoever believeth on Him may not perish, but have everlasting life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as they who looked on that serpent perished not by the serpent’s bites, so they who look in faith on Christ’s death are healed from the bites of sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those were healed from death to temporal life; whilst here He saith, “that they may have everlasting life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now there is this difference between the figurative image and the real thing: the figure procured temporal life; the reality, of which that was the figure, procures eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For God sent not His Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world through Him may be saved.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So far, then, as it lies in the physician, He is come to heal the sick.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that will not observe the orders of the physician destroys himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is come a Saviour to the world: why is he called the Saviour of the world, but that He is come to save the world, not to judge the world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt not be saved by Him; thou shalt be judged of thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why do I say, “shall be judged”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See what He says: “He that believeth on Him is not judged, but he that believeth not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What dost thou expect He is going to say, but “is judged”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Already,” saith He, “has been judged.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The judgment has not yet appeared, but already it has taken place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord knoweth them that are His: He knows who are persevering for the crown, and who for the flame; knows the wheat on His threshing-floor, and knows the chaff; knows the good corn, and knows the tares.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that believeth not is already judged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because he has not believed in the name of the only-begotten Son of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And this is the judgment, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, whose works does the Lord find to be good?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The works of none: He finds the works of all evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is it, then, that some have done the truth, and are come to the light?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is what follows: “But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what way have some done a good work to come to the light, namely, to Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how have some loved darkness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He finds all men sinners, and healeth all of sin, and that serpent in which the Lord’s death was figured healed them that were bitten, and on account of the serpent’s bite the serpent was set up, namely, the Lord’s death on account of mortal men, whom He finds unrighteous; how are we to understand that “this is the judgment, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whose works, in fact, are good?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast Thou not come to justify the ungodly?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But they loved,” saith He, “darkness rather than light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There He laid the emphasis: for many loved their sins; many confessed their sins; and he who confesses his sins, and accuses them, doth now work with God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God accuses thy sins: and if thou also accusest, thou art united to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are, as it were, two things, man and sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That thou art called man, is God’s doing; that thou art called sinner, is man’s own doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Blot out what thou hast done, that God may save what He has done.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It behoves thee to hate thine own work in thee, and to love the work of God in thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when thy own deeds will begin to displease thee, from that time thy good works begin, as thou findest fault with thy evil works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The confession of evil works is the beginning of good works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou doest the truth, and comest to the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is it thou doest the truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou dost not caress, nor soothe, nor flatter thyself; nor say, “I am righteous,” whilst thou art unrighteous: thus, thou beginnest to do the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou comest to the light, that thy works may be made manifest that they are wrought in God; for thy sin, the very thing that has given thee displeasure, would not have displeased thee, if God did not shine into thee, and His truth show it thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he that loves his sins, even after being admonished, hates the light admonishing him, and flees from it, that his works which he loves may not be proved to be evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he that doeth truth accuses his evil works in himself, spares not himself, forgives not himself, that God may forgive him: for that which he desires God to forgive, he himself acknowledges, and he comes to the light; to which he is thankful for showing him what he should hate in himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says to God, “Turn away Thy face from my sins:” yet with what countenance says it, unless he adds, “For I acknowledge mine iniquity, and my sin is ever before me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be that before thyself which thou desirest not to be before God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou wilt put thy sin behind thee, God will thrust it back before thine eyes; and this He will do at a time when there will be no more fruit of repentance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Run, my brethren, lest the darkness lay hold of you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Awake to your salvation, awake while there is time; let none be kept back from the temple of God, none kept back from the work of the Lord, none called away from continual prayer, none be defrauded of wonted devotion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Awake, then, while it is day: the day shines, Christ is the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is ready to forgive sins, but to them that acknowledge them; ready to punish the self-defenders, who boast that they are righteous, and think themselves to be something when they are nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he that walks in His love and mercy, even being free from those great and deadly sins, such crimes as murder, theft, adultery; still, because of those which seem to be minute sins, of tongue, or of thought, or of intemperance in things permitted, he doeth the truth in confession, and cometh to the light in good works: since many minute sins, if they be neglected, kill.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Minute are the drops that swell the rivers; minute are the grains of sand; but if much sand is put together, the heap presses and crushes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bilge-water neglected in the hold does the same thing as a rushing wave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Gradually it leaks in through the hold; and by long leaking in and no pumping out, it sinks the ship.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now what is this pumping out, but by good works, by sighing, fasting, giving, forgiving, so to effect that sins may not overwhelm us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The path of this life, however, is troublesome, full of temptations: in prosperity, let it not lift us up; in adversity, let it not crush us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who gave the happiness of this world gave it for thy comfort, not for thy ruin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, He who scourgeth thee in this life, doeth it for thy improvement, not for thy condemnation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bear the Father that corrects thee for thy training, lest thou feel the judge in punishing thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These things we tell you every day, and they must be often said, because they are good and wholesome.
